title: The Seven Points of True Love and Everlasting Wisdom
summary: The repository XML is a local copy/version of University of Michigan CME item CME00059, a keyed structural encoding of Karl Horstmann's 1888 Anglia article. Horstmann transcribed the anonymous Middle English Seven Points directly from Bodleian MS Douce 114, ff. 89v-148r; the article is not a critical conflation of the other witnesses it mentions. The work itself is an abridged, rearranged, sense-for-sense compilation from Heinrich Seuse's Latin Horologium sapientiae. A complete 774-page scan of Anglia volume 10 and a complete 704-canvas provider bundle for the near-complete CCCC MS 268 witness are cached as whole objects, with the target portions mapped inside them. For Cambrai MS 255, both complete provider-published selections are cached (eight decoration canvases and two other canvases), but those ten non-contiguous images are only partial coverage of the 63-folio codex. Digital Bodleian's complete four-image published selection for MS Douce 322 is likewise cached, but it has partial manuscript coverage and zero overlap with the target at ff. 20rb-25vb. BL Add MS 37790 has complete 487-canvas viewer coverage from front cover through spine, with the target at canvases 274-276; its images are not cached because the British Library currently designates the restored collection view-only with no download option. No public whole-object facsimile of the direct Douce 114 source was found; the Bodleian catalog and current reproduction-order routes are recorded.
review_notes: German belongs to Horstmann's introduction and notes, not to the medieval work's primary language profile.

The witness census follows Schultze 2013: six complete manuscripts, two near-complete manuscripts, six physical excerpt witnesses, and Caxton's incunable. Witness inclusion does not imply direct use by Horstmann.
Negative online-facsimile findings are date-bounded to 2026-07-12 and do not prove that no private, reading-room, microfilm, or unindexed reproduction exists.

A complete provider-published selection is not necessarily a complete manuscript facsimile: Cambrai MS 255's two public IIIF objects are cached in full, while their ten images cover only selected leaves of the 63-folio codex.

A complete provider selection with zero target overlap can still document the public surrogate boundary: all four published MS Douce 322 images are cached, but no work_portion is asserted because none images ff. 20rb-25vb.

Technical exposure through an Image API is not treated as download permission: Add MS 37790 remains linked-only because the British Library's current collection list expressly says the restored items are view-only and offer no download option.
Access and rights attach only to the named entity or component; the public-domain medieval text, provider images, modern scholarship, digital encoding, and repository artifact are separate layers.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00059
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00059.
The TEMPHEAD records a 2016 structural and proofing review; it is production history, not medieval provenance.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00059
entities.description: A publicly accessible keyed and structurally marked-up digital text of Horstmann's 1888 article, including its German editorial introduction and apparatus.
notes: The collection description says its later batches were manually keyed from public-domain editions and made available for bulk XML download.
The current Cloudflare challenge can impede automated retrieval of the item page even though indexed web text and the stable handle remain public.

entities.label: Horstmann's 1888 Douce 114 edition
alternate_labels: Orologium Sapientiae or The Seven Poyntes of Trewe Wisdom, aus MS. Douce 114

bibliographic.citation: Karl Horstmann, 'Orologium Sapientiae or The Seven Poyntes of Trewe Wisdom, aus MS. Douce 114,' Anglia: Zeitschrift fur englische Philologie 10 (1888): 323-389.
bibliographic.title: Orologium Sapientiae or The Seven Poyntes of Trewe Wisdom, aus MS. Douce 114

notes: Printed pp. 323-324 are Horstmann's German introduction; the Middle English table begins on p. 324, the translator's preface on p. 325, and the seven points run through p. 389.
Horstmann says that he replaces manuscript y used for thorn with thorn throughout.
The article ends by promising variants from another manuscript and Caxton later; those witnesses are therefore not modeled as direct upstream sources of this article.
Douce 114 is defective at the end. Horstmann marks the lost words and prints a parenthetical completion whose exact source is not stated here.

entities.label: Complete Robarts/Internet Archive scan of Anglia volume 10
entities.description: A complete 774-page scan of the bound 1888 volume, including front and back binding surfaces, preliminaries, contents, the full printed volume, blanks, and institutional matter. Horstmann's article is PDF pages 337-403, corresponding to printed pp. 323-389.
entities.reproduction_format: 774-page PDF with OCR text and scan-data derivatives
notes: The scan-data describes 780 capture leaves; six Delete leaves are omitted from access formats, producing the 774-page PDF.
The item metadata gives the generic serial date 1877, but the volume's internal title page reads X. Band, Halle a. S., Max Niemeyer, 1888.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Douce 114
entities.description: The direct manuscript base of Horstmann's edition. The Seven Points begins on f. 89v and occupies ff. 89v-148r; the codex also contains other devotional and hagiographic works and must not be reduced to the target range.

holding.notes: Bequeathed by Francis Douce to the Bodleian in 1834; earlier Beauvale Charterhouse and John Wells provenance is recorded in the catalog.

notes: Coloured capitals.
The catalog records Beauvale Charterhouse's ex-libris inscription.
Horstmann notes a hand change at the target's opening and indicates that the final words are lost with the manuscript's missing terminal leaf of the work.
The official MARCO record says 'Not available online' as of 2026-07-12.

A full reproduction request should specify all physical surfaces: outer boards/covers, spine and edges if offered, pastedowns, all front and rear flyleaves and blanks, and every recto and verso of ff. 1-152; the work association remains ff. 89v-148r.

entities.label: National Library of Wales MS Brogyntyn II.5
entities.description: A complete witness of Seven Points at ff. 1-90r, siglum A in Schultze. Its colophon shares the date 1419 with Cambrai 255 and connects the early transmission with Mount Grace; it does not by itself prove that the translation was composed there in 1419.

holding.notes: The official record directs readers to a surrogate because the original is restricted.

notes: Original parchment flyleaves are ff. 91-92; nineteenth-century paper flyleaves are ff. 93-94.

entities.label: Cambrai, Bibliotheque municipale, MS 255
entities.description: A complete Seven Points witness at ff. 1-61v, siglum C. It shares with Brogyntyn II.5 an identical colophon mentioning 1419 and Mount Grace.
notes: The two public IRHT IIIF objects contain ten non-contiguous selected images, not a complete facsimile of the 63-folio codex.

entities.label: Complete IRHT/ARCA published image selections for Cambrai MS 255
entities.description: The entire public provider offering consists of two partial IIIF objects: an eight-canvas decoration selection and a two-canvas partial selection. Together they image selected views of ff. 1, 1v, 8v, 10, 13, 33v, and 61v; they do not continuously reproduce the 63-folio manuscript.
entities.reproduction_format: two IIIF Presentation 2 manifests, containing eight and two color/grayscale canvases respectively
notes: Both provider-published selections are cached in full at an effective IIIF request size of 1600 pixels wide, but local-copy coverage remains partial because only ten non-contiguous images represent the physical codex.
The manifest's explicit CC BY-NC 3.0 license is applied conservatively despite the ARCA landing page's broader public-domain/free-of-rights wording.

entities.label: Gonville and Caius College MS 390/610
entities.description: A complete Seven Points witness at ff. 1-56v, siglum G. Horstmann mentions a Caius manuscript but explicitly says he had not yet examined it.
notes: Biblissima's manuscript page exposed no associated digitized object on 2026-07-12; no public whole-object image set was found.

entities.label: Columbia University MS Plimpton 256
entities.description: A complete Seven Points witness at ff. 93r-130v in the current Biblissima/JONAS record, siglum P. Schultze's 2013 witness list instead prints ff. 1-96v; use the current manuscript-level record when ordering and preserve the discrepancy as source history.
notes: Biblissima exposed no associated digitized object on 2026-07-12. A scholarly handlist reports selected Digital Scriptorium images of ff. 2, 27v, 77, 92, 93, 116v, and 130v-131v, but no complete public whole-object facsimile was verified.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Tanner 398
entities.description: A complete Seven Points witness at ff. 1-65v, siglum T.
notes: Biblissima exposed no associated digitized object, and an exact Digital Bodleian shelfmark API query returned zero records on 2026-07-12.

entities.label: Cambridge, Corpus Christi College, MS 268
entities.description: A near-complete Seven Points witness at ff. 54r-97r, siglum X, lacking the Translator's Preface. It forms one part of a much larger devotional miscellany and must be cached and described as the whole physical/digital object rather than as target-only leaves.

holding.notes: Parker Library, Corpus Christi College, Cambridge; the digital record is delivered by Stanford Libraries.

notes: The manuscript contains Walter Hilton's Book of Contemplation and other devotional and spiritual tracts.
A fifteenth-century inscription records ownership by Elizabeth Wylby, an Augustinian nun of Campsey Priory.

entities.label: Complete CCCC MS 268 IIIF provider object
entities.description: The full 704-canvas color sequence exposed by the Stanford/Parker IIIF Presentation 2 manifest. It contains paired non-cropped/context and tightly cropped captures for 352 physical surfaces: front inside board, front flyleaves i-iv, every recto and verso of ff. 1-169, rear flyleaves v-vi, and back inside board. The provider does not expose separate outer-board, spine, or edge views.
entities.reproduction_format: 704 IIIF color canvases; cached as manifest, inventory, and 1600-pixel JPEG bundle
notes: The manifest sequence is lexically sorted by provider filename rather than physical folio order; inventory.json preserves the exact canvas-to-local-file mapping.

The target occupies provider Pages 473-496, 501-540, 545-584, 589-628, and 633-662. Interposed Page runs are paired alternate captures of earlier-numbered folios and are retained because the bundle is whole-object.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS e Musaeo 111
entities.description: A near-complete Seven Points witness at ff. 1-34v, siglum E, lacking both the Table of Contents and the Translator's Preface.
notes: Biblissima exposed no associated digitized object, and an exact Digital Bodleian shelfmark API query returned zero records on 2026-07-12.

entities.label: British Library MS Additional 37049
entities.description: A devotional miscellany containing the Scire mori excerpt from Point V at ff. 39r-43v followed by the Formula compendiosa vite spiritualis excerpt from Point IV at ff. 43v-44v, siglum Ad. This is an excerpt witness, not a complete copy.

physical_description.extent: i + 96 leaves plus four unfoliated front and four rear flyleaves; target excerpts ff. 39r-44v

notes: The current catalog reports full digital coverage, but its linked viewer stated that digital images were unavailable on 2026-07-12; no cache was made.

entities.label: British Library MS Additional 37790
entities.description: A 238-folio Carthusian anthology containing a Point IV excerpt at ff. 135v-136v, siglum Ad2. Schultze calls the excerpt Formula compendiosa vite spiritualis; the current British Library catalog spells the title Formula compendiosa rite spiritualis.
notes: The public viewer covers the complete provider object from front cover through spine. No local manuscript-image cache was created because the British Library's current list says these restored items are view-only and offer no download option.

entities.label: Complete British Library viewer facsimile of Add MS 37790
entities.description: A 487-canvas IIIF Presentation 3 object covering the complete manuscript presentation sequence: front cover, front matter, every folio side through f. 238v, rear matter, back cover, and spine. The Seven Points excerpt is on canvases 274-276, corresponding to ff. 135v-136v.
notes: The manifest labels its rights 'Public Domain in most countries, other than the UK.' This is not treated as permission to bypass the Library's explicit view-only/no-download service condition.
No local_copy is present by design; use the exact manifest and canvas links recorded under access.

entities.label: British Library Digitised manuscripts and archives list
entities.description: The complete 261-page official list governing the restored manuscript viewer collection. Page 1 states that items are view-only with no download option; Add MS 37790 appears on page 29.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.5.45
entities.description: An excerpt witness of Scire mori from Point V at ff. 14r-22v, siglum Ff.
notes: Biblissima exposed no associated digitized object, and exact CUDL shelfmark searches produced no public object on 2026-07-12.

entities.label: British Library MS Harley 1706
entities.description: An excerpt witness of Scire mori from Point V at ff. 20ra-24va, siglum Hrl.
notes: The current British Library catalog marks the legacy digitized content unavailable and displays 'Images currently unavailable' on 2026-07-12.

entities.label: Bodleian Library MS Douce 322
entities.description: An excerpt witness of Scire mori from Point V at ff. 20rb-25vb, siglum D2. Horstmann mentions this copy in a footnote, but it is not the base of his complete Seven Points text.

date_statements.label: manuscript production
date_statements.value: third quarter of the fifteenth century

notes: Digital Bodleian exposes only four selected images (f. 15r, f. 19v, and two captures labeled f. 78r). None images the target ff. 20rb-25vb, and the public record declares its completeness partial.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian selected images of MS Douce 322
entities.description: A partial four-canvas IIIF object containing f. 15r, f. 19v, and two captures labeled f. 78r. It is neither a whole-manuscript facsimile nor an image source for the Seven Points excerpt at ff. 20rb-25vb.
notes: The complete four-image provider selection is cached locally. It remains partial manuscript coverage and has zero target overlap; the whole-codex request route is recorded separately.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Hh.1.11
entities.description: An excerpt witness of the Treatise on the Eucharist from Point VI at ff. 45-53v, siglum Hh.
notes: An exact CUDL shelfmark search produced no public object on 2026-07-12; Cambridge University Library's whole-object imaging request route is recorded under access.

entities.label: Caxton's Seven Points in The Book of Divers Ghostly Matters
entities.description: The first printed edition, Westminster, 1491, STC 3305, fols. 1-96v. The schema has no incunable/early-print entity class, so this is typed as scholarly_edition with an explicit modeling note.

bibliographic.citation: The Book of Divers Ghostly Matters (Westminster: William Caxton, 1491), STC 3305, Seven Points at fols. 1-96v.
bibliographic.title: The Book of Divers Ghostly Matters

notes: Schultze reports that Point V follows an excerpt branch close to Douce 322, Harley 1706, or Ff.5.45, while the remainder is close to Brogyntyn II.5. These are textual affinities, not proof of direct copying from a particular extant object.

entities.label: Schultze 2013 witness and paratext study
entities.description: A peer-reviewed study that defines the anonymous compilation, date and source relationship, analyzes its ordinatio and Latin paratexts, and supplies the complete/near-complete/excerpt witness census used here.

bibliographic.citation: Dirk Schultze, 'Wisdom in the Margins: Text and Paratext in The Seven Points of True Love and Everlasting Wisdom,' Etudes anglaises 66, no. 3 (2013): 341-356, doi:10.3917/etan.663.0341.
bibliographic.title: Wisdom in the Margins: Text and Paratext in The Seven Points of True Love and Everlasting Wisdom

work_portion.label: Horstmann's Seven Points article
notes: The medieval table begins on printed p. 324/PDF p. 338; printed p. 323/PDF p. 337 is Horstmann's German title and introduction.
The article ends on printed p. 389/PDF p. 403 with the defective explicit, editorial note, Berlin dateline, and Horstmann signature.
All 774 access-format pages were retained, including binding surfaces, preliminaries, blanks, the entire volume, and matter adjacent to the article.
The internal title page identifies Anglia volume 10 as published in 1888.

local_copies.label: Internet Archive item metadata
notes: Preserves provider file inventory and provenance metadata; its generic serial date 1877 is contradicted by the scanned volume title page.

work_portion.label: OCR corresponding to Horstmann's article
notes: OCR is a derivative aid and does not preserve typographic or manuscript features reliably; consult the PDF images for editorial italics and supplied text.

work_portion.label: Page mapping for Horstmann's article
locators: leafNum 337 / printed p. 323 through leafNum 403 / printed p. 389

notes: Cached files are gitignored research copies; the manifest records exact provider download URLs and hashes.

access.access_method: MARCO record with links to Bodleian finding aids
access.format: HTML catalog record

access.access_method: Follow the MARCO record's request link and Bodleian special-collections reader procedures
access.contact: Bodleian Special Collections; specialcollections@bodleian.ox.ac.uk for manuscript queries
access.format: onsite manuscript consultation subject to repository conditions

access.access_method: Complete the mediated-copying PDF form and email it, or mail it to Reader Services, Weston Library
access.contact: mediated.copying@bodleian.ox.ac.uk; postal route: Reader Services, Weston Library, Bodleian Libraries, Broad Street, Oxford OX1 3BG, UK
access.format: reference-quality PDF scans; GBP 1 per scan for non-Oxford users, GBP 10 minimum, subject to condition and capacity
notes: The service is available to all, but the library may refuse or limit scanning for conservation, legal, or capacity reasons.

Request the complete physical object, not only ff. 89v-148r: all boards/covers, spine and edges if offered, pastedowns, front/rear flyleaves and blanks, and every side of ff. 1-152. Identify ff. 89v-148r separately as the work portion.

Mediated-copying images are intended for research; out-of-copyright scans are supplied under CC BY-NC 4.0 according to the current form. Commercial or other non-academic reproduction requires Imaging Services permission.

access.access_method: Submit the Imaging Services image order form by email or post
access.contact: imaging@bodleian.ox.ac.uk; Imaging Services, Weston Library, Broad Street, Oxford OX1 3BG, UK
access.format: high-quality color TIFF; current form lists GBP 35 for 1-2 images, GBP 7.50 each for subsequent images 3-99, and GBP 6.15 each for subsequent 100+ images, subject to quotation
notes: A complete color codex order would involve hundreds of images and may therefore be very expensive; obtain a quotation rather than extrapolating a guaranteed total from the published bands.
Commercial, print-on-demand, repository, cover, or other non-academic reuse must be cleared under the order terms and any separate permission/licensing process.

access.access_method: Digital Bodleian shelfmark search and MARCO availability statement
access.format: no public whole-object image set found
notes: All 506 Digital Bodleian results for shelfmark:Douce were checked across six result pages; MS Douce 114 was absent although nearby shelfmarks were present.
This dated negative does not establish that no private, microfilm, or unindexed reproduction exists.

local_copies.label: Complete CCCC MS 268 IIIF provider bundle
notes: All 704 canvases in the provider manifest were retained: paired non-cropped/context and tightly cropped captures of the front inside board, flyleaves i-iv, fols. 1-169 recto and verso, flyleaves v-vi, and back inside board. The provider supplies no separate outer-board, spine, or edge views.
The manifest sequence is lexical by provider filename rather than physical folio order; inventory.json records each original canvas URL, requested image URL, member path, byte count, and SHA-256.
The ZIP contains manifest.json, inventory.json, and all 704 JPEGs, for 706 members total.
Effective IIIF request profile: size 1600,; format jpg.

work_portion.label: The Seven Points of True Love and Everlasting Wisdom
notes: The target begins in the lower portion of f. 54r after preceding material; the page includes its red heading and opening.
The target ends with its red trailer on f. 97r. F. 97v is blank apart from bleed-through and remains in the whole-object bundle.

Each physical surface has NC and TC variants. Interposed provider Pages 497-500, 541-544, 585-588, and 629-632 are paired captures of fols. 5-8, not target leaves; they remain because the complete provider object is cached.

The cached bundle is gitignored. The CC BY-NC 4.0 license permits attributed noncommercial use but does not authorize commercial/POD redistribution; contact Parker Library for publication-quality or commercial permission.

ARCA's landing page describes the medieval work as public domain/free of rights, while both image manifests explicitly license their files CC BY-NC 3.0. Apply the narrower image license unless the provider clarifies otherwise.

The current ARCA record also emits manifest IDs fdt424dpoqsq and fyrjndzsb92i. They expose the same canvas IDs, Image API service IDs, and CC BY-NC 3.0 license as the cached Biblissima-linked manifests, so duplicate image bundles were not downloaded.
The cached bundles are gitignored and do not authorize commercial or print-on-demand image redistribution.

access.contact: photo@lib.cam.ac.uk; Cultural Heritage Imaging Laboratory, Cambridge University Library, West Road, Cambridge CB3 9DR, UK
access.format: normally uncompressed JPG at 600 ppi or best possible; TIFF on request, subject to condition
notes: Request the complete physical codex, including binding matter, endleaves, blanks, and every folio side, and separately identify ff. 14r-22v as the work portion.
The current service page gives a GBP 25 excluding-VAT minimum and supplies a quotation before purchase.
Choose the all-other-uses route if repository publication or commercial/POD use is contemplated; permission and image-supply fees are distinct.

access.access_method: British Library Archives and Manuscripts record
access.format: HTML catalog record; legacy digital images unavailable
notes: The current catalog marks the legacy digitized content unavailable. Request the whole manuscript rather than only ff. 20ra-24va if a reproduction service becomes available.

access.access_method: British Library catalog's legacy-digitization link/status
access.format: legacy digital facsimile currently unavailable
notes: The catalog displayed 'Images currently unavailable' and classed the content under BL legacy Digitised Manuscripts (unavailable).

access.access_method: Public scholarly census plus Bodleian reproduction service
access.format: HTML census; repository inquiry/order route
notes: Request the complete manuscript if practical and identify ff. 20rb-25vb as the target; the public four-image object omits the target.

access.access_method: Digital Bodleian object and IIIF Presentation 2 manifest
access.format: four selected color IIIF canvases; target absent

local_copies.label: Complete Digital Bodleian published selection for MS Douce 322
notes: The ZIP preserves manifest.json, inventory.json, and all four provider-published canvases, for six members total.
Effective IIIF request profile: size 1600,; format jpg.
Complete provider-published selection but partial manuscript coverage; canvas labels are f. 15r, f. 19v, and two captures labeled f. 78r.
Zero target overlap: Seven Points is ff. 20rb-25vb and no public canvas images that range.
The provider marks completeness partial. Canvas labels are f. 15r, f. 19v, and two captures labeled f. 78r; target ff. 20rb-25vb is not imaged.

The complete public four-image selection is cached even though it is not a whole-manuscript facsimile and has zero target overlap. Request the whole codex through the Bodleian's mediated-copying service if practical.

access.access_method: Public scholarly census giving repository, shelfmark, and target locus
access.format: HTML witness list
notes: Contact Cambridge University Library for whole-object access/reproduction; target excerpt ff. 45-53v.

access.access_method: Exact Cambridge Digital Library shelfmark audit
access.format: no public whole-object image set found
notes: Exact public-index searches returned no CUDL object; this is a dated negative and does not rule out private or microfilm surrogates.

access.access_method: Submit Cambridge University Library's online Imaging and Permissions request form

access.format: normally uncompressed JPG at 600 ppi or best possible; TIFF on request, subject to condition
notes: Request the complete physical codex, including binding matter, endleaves, blanks, and every folio side, and separately identify ff. 45-53v as the work portion.
The current service page gives a GBP 25 excluding-VAT minimum and supplies a quotation before purchase.
Choose the all-other-uses route if repository publication or commercial/POD use is contemplated; permission and image-supply fees are distinct.

access.access_method: Public scholarly census giving imprint, STC number, and locus
access.format: HTML article and witness list
notes: A complete public scan was not independently verified and cached in this pass; search STC 3305 and The Book of Divers Ghostly Matters in institutional incunable repositories.

rights.basis: The paired U-M collection asserts No Copyright - United States and says the later corpus texts were manually keyed from public-domain editions. The repository artifact is a copied/versioned representation of that encoding; no separate repository license was found in this review.
notes: Do not extend this statement to manuscript photography, modern scholarship, or provider files.

rights.basis: The U-M CME collection states that its materials are in the public domain in the United States and displays the No Copyright - United States rights statement.

rights.component: anonymous fifteenth-century Middle English textual content of Seven Points
rights.jurisdiction: United States and jurisdictions with ordinary authorial copyright terms
rights.basis: The anonymous work and this manuscript witness date from the fifteenth century, many centuries beyond ordinary copyright terms. This does not determine rights or contractual conditions in a modern photograph or scan.

rights.component: 1888 editorial text, transcription, introduction, apparatus, and typography
rights.basis: First published in 1888, well before the current United States public-domain cutoff for published works.

rights.component: Robarts/Internet Archive scan and provider derivative files
rights.basis: The item provides public downloads but its metadata contains no item-specific licenseurl or rights field. The public-domain status of the underlying 1888 edition is recorded separately and is not automatically a license statement for provider files.
notes: Keep the cached whole-volume scan gitignored unless redistribution terms are independently established. Exact public download links remain in access metadata.

rights.jurisdiction: United States and jurisdictions with ordinary authorial copyright terms
rights.basis: The manuscript and anonymous target text date from the fifteenth century; the provider image license is a separate layer.

rights.component: Stanford/Parker IIIF manuscript images and cached complete bundle
rights.basis: The IIIF manifest and MODS record license the images under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International and direct publication-quality or commercial requests to the Parker Library.
notes: Attribution and noncommercial conditions apply; the license has no ShareAlike term.

Publishing the cached images in a repository designed to enable commercial/POD use is not authorized by CC BY-NC without additional permission. Keep the bundle gitignored and link users to the provider.
Contact parker-library@corpus.cam.ac.uk for higher-resolution images or commercial/publication permission.

rights.component: fifteenth-century manuscript text and anonymous Seven Points content
rights.jurisdiction: United States and jurisdictions with ordinary authorial copyright terms
rights.basis: The anonymous work and manuscript are fifteenth-century; Biblissima/ARCA also labels the underlying work public domain/free of rights. Rights and terms in modern images are separate.

rights.component: IRHT/ARCA IIIF images and both cached complete provider selections
rights.basis: Both IIIF manifests explicitly license the image objects under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 3.0 Unported, although the ARCA landing page separately describes the underlying work as public domain/free of rights.
notes: Treat the manifest's narrower, file-specific CC BY-NC 3.0 statement as controlling unless IRHT/Le Labo clarifies the landing-page conflict.
The license permits attributed noncommercial reuse but does not authorize image use in commercial/POD outputs; keep the bundles gitignored.

rights.component: fifteenth-century manuscript text and anonymous Seven Points excerpt
rights.jurisdiction: United States and jurisdictions with ordinary authorial copyright terms
rights.basis: The anonymous excerpt and manuscript are fifteenth-century; this does not determine rights or access conditions in modern images.

rights.jurisdiction: Most countries other than the United Kingdom
rights.basis: The IIIF manifest's item-specific rights field states 'Public Domain in most countries, other than the UK.'
notes: The service nevertheless designates the restored items view-only and says there is no download option. Public-domain status is not treated as authorization for systematic extraction contrary to that access condition.

rights.basis: The manifest expressly excludes the United Kingdom from its public-domain statement and gives no affirmative UK reuse license.
notes: No manuscript images were cached. Seek British Library guidance before UK publication, repository redistribution, or commercial/POD use.

rights.component: British Library compiled PDF list and layout
rights.basis: The official PDF is publicly downloadable, but it carries no item-specific open redistribution license in the file reviewed.
notes: The complete supporting PDF is retained only in gitignored research storage; link to the official download rather than committing the file.

rights.component: four selected Digital Bodleian manuscript images and the complete cached provider-selection bundle
rights.basis: The IIIF manifest attribution supplies the images under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International.
notes: The public images omit the target work. Keep the bundle gitignored; commercial/POD image use requires additional permission.

rights.component: future reference scans supplied by Bodleian mediated copying
rights.jurisdiction: United Kingdom and use governed by provider terms
rights.basis: The current mediated-copying form states that scans of out-of-copyright material are copyright Bodleian Libraries and supplied under CC BY-NC 4.0; the Imaging Services terms govern reproduction.
notes: This prospective statement describes current standard terms, not rights in a file not yet received; confirm the actual order invoice, delivery notice, and license.

Noncommercial research use and qualifying academic publication are treated differently from commercial, print-on-demand, repository, marketing, or cover uses. Obtain written permission where the planned repository context is uncertain.

rights.component: 2013 article text, layout, and provider files
rights.basis: A modern scholarly article is within ordinary copyright terms, and no open redistribution license was displayed on the public HTML page.
notes: Facts and citations may be summarized with attribution; do not redistribute the article or treat public HTML access as an open license.

rights.basis: The edition was first printed in 1491. Rights in any modern photograph, scan, transcription, or editorial apparatus must be evaluated separately.

editorial_practices.representation: italic type for expansions; parentheses for some supplied material
editorial_practices.description: Horstmann's transcription uses italics for expanded letters and abbreviations and parentheses for editorial supply. The CME XML preserves these italics as HI REND='italic', so italics are documentary editorial signals rather than emphasis.
editorial_practices.scope: Middle English transcription on printed pp. 324-389

editorial_practices.source_feature: Seven Points embedded within a 152-leaf devotional codex
editorial_practices.representation: article prints only the target work with a short editorial introduction
editorial_practices.description: The article selects the Seven Points from Douce 114 rather than reproducing the whole manuscript; adjacent works, binding matter, flyleaves, blanks, and most physical layout are absent.

editorial_practices.source_feature: base-manuscript readings, corrections, lacunae, and hand changes
editorial_practices.representation: German and abbreviated footnotes using labels such as ms., l., and fehlt; final missing words marked and partly supplied
editorial_practices.description: The notes document readings and editorial interventions against Douce 114, but this installment is not a full multi-witness critical collation: Horstmann promises other-manuscript and Caxton variants later.

evidence.citation: General Corpus / Kyvero-Press CME source XML, CME00059.xml.
evidence.locator: IDG CME00059; DIV1 and ten DIV2 elements
evidence.summary: Provides the exact repository artifact, encoded Horstmann text, source and editorial statements, page milestones, notes, final lacuna, and production history.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection page and Rights and Permissions.
evidence.summary: Describes public cross-searchable access, manual keying from public-domain editions, bulk XML availability, and the No Copyright - United States rights statement.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Middle English Compendium, CME file and URL mapping.
evidence.locator: CME00059.xml row
evidence.summary: Maps CME00059 to 'Orologium sapientiae, or the seven poyntes of trewe wisdom,' edited by K. Horstmann in Anglia 10 (1888).

evidence.citation: Karl Horstmann, 'Orologium Sapientiae or The Seven Poyntes of Trewe Wisdom, aus MS. Douce 114,' Anglia 10 (1888): 323-389; complete Robarts scan.
evidence.locator: printed pp. 323-389; PDF pp. 337-403
evidence.summary: Confirms the internal 1888 date, article boundaries, direct Douce 114 source, editorial y-to-thorn normalization, abbreviation typography, apparatus, incomplete ending, and closing promise of later variants.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for angliazeitschrif10tbuoft.
evidence.summary: Provides the exact PDF/OCR/scan-data filenames, Robarts contributor, Toronto scanner, public download routes, and absence of item-specific licenseurl and rights fields.
notes: The generic date 1877 conflicts with the volume's scanned internal title page and is not used as the article date.

evidence.citation: Visual and page-level inspection of the cached complete Anglia volume 10 PDF and scan-data.
evidence.locator: PDF pp. 1, 7, 337, 403, and 774; scan-data leafNums 337 and 403
evidence.summary: Verified front/back binding surfaces, the 1888 internal title page, article start and end, and the exact printed-page-to-PDF mapping 323-389 to 337-403.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Manuscripts and Archives at Oxford University, MS Douce 114.
evidence.locator: work beginning f. 89v
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, date, language, parchment support, ii + 152-leaf extent, coloured capitals, Beauvale and Douce provenance, Seven Points title and incipits, and 'Not available online' status.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian complete shelfmark:Douce result audit.
evidence.summary: All 506 shelfmark:Douce results were checked over six 100-row pages on 2026-07-12. Exact MS Douce 114 was absent, while nearby Douce shelfmarks appeared.
notes: A dated public-index negative only; not proof that no private or unindexed reproduction exists.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Special Collections Mediated Copying.
evidence.summary: States that the service is available to all, supplies reference-quality PDF scans, charges non-Oxford users GBP 1 per scan, averages ten working days, and may decline material for legal, conservation, or other reasons.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries Mediated Copying Order Form.
evidence.summary: Provides email and postal submission routes, the GBP 1-per-scan and GBP 10-minimum fee, order fields, research/reproduction declaration, and CC BY-NC 4.0 statement for scans of out-of-copyright material.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries Imaging Services Image Order Form.
evidence.locator: pp. 1-6, especially fee schedule p. 6
evidence.summary: Provides email/post ordering, high-quality color TIFF delivery, copyright declaration, and current per-image fee bands for standard and specialist photography.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries Imaging Services, Reproducing Images.
evidence.summary: States that supplied images are governed by terms of use; noncommercial and qualifying academic uses are permitted, academic print runs below 3,000 need no separate library permission, and non-academic reproduction requires a separate permission application and credit.

evidence.citation: Dirk Schultze, 'Wisdom in the Margins: Text and Paratext in The Seven Points of True Love and Everlasting Wisdom,' Etudes anglaises 66, no. 3 (2013): 341-356, doi:10.3917/etan.663.0341.
evidence.locator: paragraphs 4-17; Early prints, manuscripts, and sigla
evidence.summary: Grounds the work boundary, circa-1400/1419 date, Mount Grace and female-patron qualifications, source adaptation, seven-point structure, paratextual analysis, and the complete, near-complete, excerpt, and Caxton witness census with loci.

evidence.citation: Stanford Digital Repository / Parker Library, MODS record for CCCC MS 268.
evidence.summary: Provides title, circa-1450 summary, broad 1400-1499 range, vellum support, 265 x 180 mm dimensions, 34-line layout, foliation ff. i-iv + 1-169 + v-vi, contents/provenance context, repository, and CC BY-NC 4.0 terms.

evidence.citation: Stanford/Parker IIIF Presentation 2 manifest for CCCC MS 268.
evidence.summary: Exposes 704 canvases, public Image API services, manifest attribution, and CC BY-NC 4.0 license. The complete cached bundle preserves the manifest and all canvases at the recorded request size.

evidence.citation: Visual and inventory audit of the complete CCCC MS 268 IIIF bundle.
evidence.locator: provider Pages 473-496, 501-540, 545-584, 589-628, and 633-662; first/last and physical-surface sequence
evidence.summary: Verified 704 local images, target beginning low on f. 54r and ending with the trailer on f. 97r, blank f. 97v retention, paired NC/TC variants, and whole provider-object coverage from inside boards through flyleaves and all folio sides.

evidence.citation: National Library of Wales Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Seven Points of True Love, Brogyntyn II.5.
evidence.summary: Describes the complete mid-fifteenth-century parchment witness, target ff. 1r-90r, physical extent and flyleaves, and restricted-original/surrogate access condition.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Add MS 37049.
evidence.summary: Provides date 1460-1500, material and extent, public catalog access, a full-digital-coverage assertion, and the viewer link that reported digital images unavailable on the check date.

evidence.citation: Biblissima+/JONAS, Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS e Musaeo 111.
evidence.summary: Identifies the manuscript, holding institution, anonymous Seven Points content, and target folios 1-34; its Mirador control reported that the exact selection contained no digitized object on the access date.

evidence.citation: Biblissima+/JONAS, New York, Columbia University, Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Plimpton MS 256.
evidence.summary: Identifies the complete Seven Points witness at ff. 93r-130v, late-fifteenth-century date, siglum P, and the preceding devotional contents; this current locus conflicts with Schultze's printed ff. 1-96v citation. Its Mirador control reported that the exact selection contained no digitized object on the access date.

evidence.citation: Biblissima+/JONAS/ARCA, Cambrai, Le Labo, MS 255 (245).
evidence.locator: 63-folio manuscript; Seven Points ff. 1-61v; two partial digitization links
evidence.summary: Provides shelfmark variants, 63-folio parchment extent, 219 x 152 mm dimensions, 1419-1475 date range, target work and colophon, two partial IIIF selections, and an underlying-work statement of public domain/free of rights.

evidence.citation: IRHT-CNRS/ARCA IIIF manifests for the Cambrai MS 255 decoration and partial image selections.
evidence.locator: eight decoration canvases plus two partial canvases; selected ff. 1, 1v, 8v, 10, 13, 33v, and 61v
evidence.summary: The first manifest exposes eight canvases and the second two, each with public Image API services, attribution to Le Labo - Cambrai, and an explicit CC BY-NC 3.0 license. Current ARCA IDs fdt424dpoqsq and fyrjndzsb92i duplicate the same canvas and image-service sets; the cached Biblissima-linked IDs preserve the exact download provenance. Together they are only selected coverage of the 63-folio codex.

evidence.citation: Inventory, integrity, dimension, and visual audit of both complete cached Cambrai MS 255 provider selections.
evidence.locator: all 8/8 decoration images and 2/2 partial images; visually checked f. 1, f. 33v, f. 10, and f. 61v
evidence.summary: Verified complete provider-object counts, ZIP integrity, 1600-pixel effective requests, non-contiguous manuscript coverage, the table opening on f. 1, target text at f. 10 and f. 33v, and the explicit plus 1419 Mount Grace colophon on f. 61v.
notes: The second audited bundle is source-cache/CME00059/cambrai-ms-255-partial-selection-complete-iiif-1600px.zip, SHA-256 f7d87e970b1e40ef24ffd6af8a6b5209b7ec5364c50ebe8a4f51287a30ab4f24.

evidence.citation: Biblissima+/JONAS, Cambridge, Gonville and Caius College, MS 390/610.
evidence.locator: Seven Points ff. 1r-56v
evidence.summary: Identifies the complete witness, target locus, mid-fifteenth-century date, and Biblissima identifier Q269585; its Mirador control reported no digitized object for the exact selection.

evidence.citation: Biblissima+/JONAS, Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Tanner 398.
evidence.locator: Seven Points ff. 1-65v
evidence.summary: Identifies the complete witness, target locus, scribe Johannes P., and Biblissima identifier Q274569; its Mirador control reported no digitized object for the exact selection.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian exact metadata-only shelfmark API audit for MS Tanner 398 and MS e Musaeo 111.
evidence.summary: The discovery API returned totalItems 0 for both exact shelfmark queries on 2026-07-12. These are dated public-index negatives, not proof against private or unindexed surrogates.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Add MS 37790, the Amherst Manuscript.
evidence.summary: Confirms the 238-folio parchment codex, middle-fifteenth-century date, complete digital-content link, and the Point IV excerpt at ff. 135v-136v under the catalog title Formula compendiosa rite spiritualis.

evidence.citation: British Library IIIF Presentation 3 manifest for Add MS 37790.
evidence.locator: 487 canvases; target canvases 274-276 (ff. 135v-136v)
evidence.summary: Exposes a complete viewer sequence from front cover through spine, exact target canvases, and the item-specific rights statement 'Public Domain in most countries, other than the UK.'

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitised manuscripts and archives, complete downloadable list.
evidence.locator: PDF p. 1 access notice; PDF p. 29 Add MS 37790
evidence.summary: The current 261-page official list says the restored items are view-only and have no download option, and it specifically lists Add MS 37790. The complete supporting PDF was cached; manuscript images were not.

evidence.citation: Biblissima+/JONAS, Cambridge University Library, MS Ff.5.45.
evidence.locator: Seven Points excerpt ff. 14r-22v
evidence.summary: Identifies the witness, target excerpt, and Biblissima identifier Q269842; its Mirador control reported no digitized object for the exact selection.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Digital Library exact shelfmark searches for CUL MSS Ff.5.45 and Hh.1.11.
evidence.summary: Exact shelfmark queries and exact-domain index searches produced no public CUDL objects on 2026-07-12. The finding is date-bounded and does not rule out private or microfilm surrogates.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Image Supply and Reproduction.
evidence.summary: Provides the online request route, private-study and publication/commercial choices, whole-item quotation process, standard delivery specifications, GBP 25 excluding-VAT minimum, condition review, contact email, and separate permission requirements.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 1706.
evidence.locator: complete manuscript and ff. 3r-95v component; target ff. 20ra-24va
evidence.summary: Describes the 216-folio devotional miscellany and currently marks the legacy digitized content and images unavailable.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian partial object and IIIF manifest for MS Douce 322.
evidence.locator: four canvases: f. 15r, f. 19v, and two captures labeled f. 78r
evidence.summary: The public object declares completeness partial, provides four selected images under CC BY-NC 4.0 attribution, and omits the Seven Points excerpt at ff. 20rb-25vb.

evidence.citation: Inventory, integrity, dimension, and visual audit of the complete cached Digital Bodleian provider selection for MS Douce 322.
evidence.locator: all 4/4 provider images: f. 15r, f. 19v, and two captures labeled f. 78r
evidence.summary: Verified all four provider canvases, six-member ZIP integrity, 1600-pixel effective requests, and selected manuscript content. None of the published images overlaps the target at ff. 20rb-25vb, so the cache has no work_portion mapping.

open_questions.question: Will the Bodleian supply an affordable complete color facsimile of MS Douce 114 including all binding matter, endleaves, blanks, and folios?
next_steps: Submit a whole-object request or ask Imaging Services and mediated copying for a conservation assessment and quotation.
Specify every desired physical surface and separately identify Seven Points at ff. 89v-148r.
If supplied, cache the complete delivered object locally, record exact delivery/source URLs and terms, and add a local_copy work_portion mapping before any reuse.

open_questions.question: Would the Bodleian permit a received Douce 114 facsimile to be redistributed in this repository or used in a project that enables commercial print-on-demand publication?
next_steps: Ask Imaging Services in writing, describing the public Git repository, non-facsimile research use, and possible commercial/POD downstream context separately.
Treat standard CC BY-NC research-scan terms as insufficient for commercial/POD redistribution unless the Bodleian grants additional permission.
Retain the delivered facsimile only in gitignored local storage until written terms are clear.

open_questions.question: What exact physical loss causes Douce 114's defective ending, and what witness or inference supplied Horstmann's parenthetical completion?
next_steps: Inspect the complete physical/facsimile sequence around ff. 148r-152v, including stubs, quire boundaries, blanks, and endleaves.
Compare the parenthetical text with complete witnesses and Caxton, but do not identify a source without documentary evidence.

open_questions.question: Which physical Latin Horologium manuscript or textual branch did the anonymous translator-compiler use?
next_steps: Use modern source analysis and the translator's rearranged chapter mappings to identify textual affinities without promoting a physical exemplar prematurely.
Keep Heinrich Seuse's abstract source work separate from any later critical edition or surviving Latin witness.
